preprocessing.cleanedTranscriptSummary: User discussed practical ways to use GitHub Copilot in daily workflows, compared prompt styles, and highlighted repeatable patterns that improved output quality.

primaryTopic.value: Using GitHub Copilot effectively in daily workflows with structured prompts

problemStatement.value: People use AI tools inconsistently and get weak results because prompts are vague, context is missing, and outputs are not validated.

keyTakeaways.value: Better results come from giving Copilot role plus goal plus constraints in one prompt.
Short iterative prompts outperform one large prompt when refining practical outputs.
Adding examples of desired output format reduces ambiguity and rework.
Users should validate AI outputs against real-world constraints before acting.

evidenceSnippets.quote: When I gave context, constraints, and expected format together, the answer quality improved immediately.
Single giant prompts produced generic advice; iterative prompts gave practical steps.

risksUnknowns.value: No quantified before and after metric for productivity gain was provided.

handoff.notesForWriter: Use plain language and non-technical examples first; include one short framework for writing effective prompts.
